toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Changelog

All notable changes to TinyRaven are documented here. Format follows
[Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/); this project adheres to
[Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [0.3.2] — 2026-06-30

### Changed
- Auto-deploy on release is now **version-tagged via the Dokploy API** (`compose.update` env → `compose.deploy`) instead of a webhook on `:latest`. A version tag is a new image reference, so Dokploy pulls the new digest every time; the `:latest` webhook recreated from cache and silently never updated.

## [0.3.1] — 2026-06-30

### Added
- API read endpoints toward full Tinybird `/v0` parity: `GET /v0/datasources`, `GET /v0/datasources/{name}`, `GET /v0/pipes`, `GET /v0/pipes/{name}` (all ADMIN), plus pipe output formats `GET /v0/pipes/{name}.{csv,ndjson}` (READ-scoped) alongside the existing `.json`.
- Distribution: signed APT + RPM repos published to GitHub Pages (`apt-get install tinyraven`); GoReleaser Scoop/AUR/Nix/winget publisher blocks (fail-safe — push only when their token exists); install matrix + `docs/install.md`.
- `docs/parity-gaps.md` ranked `/v0` parity audit.

### Changed
- Prod deploy model: image tags pinned to `latest`; release builds `:version` + `:latest` then triggers the Dokploy deploy webhook (`pull_policy: always` recreates on the new digest). Dokploy panel env is the documented source of truth.
- APT/RPM gh-pages publish now does a scoped `rsync --delete` of `apt/`+`rpm/` (keep-2 pool prune actually removes old blobs); `helm-publish` + `apt-publish` share a concurrency group to avoid gh-pages races.

### Tests
- `internal/apierr` and `internal/model` 0→100%; `internal/pipe` 86.7→96% (error-envelope mapping, `Token.HasScope` auth gate, registry hot-reload swap, control-flow branch + non-boolean-condition rejection).

### Security
- Rotated the admin token (purged the old value from the Dokploy env and Redis `tr:token:`).

## [0.3.0] — 2026-06-30

### Added
- Control-flow pipe templating: `{% if/elif/else/end %}` + `defined()` via expr-lang (ADR 0003).
- Resource-token materialization: `TOKEN "x" READ/APPEND` in files → scoped tokens on `tr deploy` (ADR 0030).
- `tr deploy --check` (dry run), `tr login`, `tr status`.
- Per-pipe `RATE_LIMIT` enforcement (token+pipe; ADR 0015); `/tr/v1/docs` UI (off by default, `TR_DOCS_ENABLED`; ADR 0017); dedicated read-only ClickHouse user (ADR 0011).
- Release signing (GPG) + Helm chart published to GitHub Pages.

### Changed
- ponytail cuts: strconv.Itoa over hand-rolled, merged CHPinger into Pinger, dropped dead Connector field.

## [0.2.0] — 2026-06-29

Phases 3–5 + scoped auth + a query-path perf fix.

### Added
- **Branching** (Phase 3): `tr_{branch}` ClickHouse DB per git branch; `tr deploy --branch` / `tr local start --branch`.
- **Materialized views** + **breaking migrations** (shadow → backfill → `EXCHANGE TABLES`, `--allow-breaking`).
- **Config**: `~/.tinyraven/config.yml` (Tinybird-compatible) + `TINYBIRD_HOST/TOKEN/WORKSPACE` env.
- **Connectors** (Phase 5): Kafka/S3/PostgreSQL as ClickHouse-native engines in `.datasource` (ADR 0019) + templates; BI-tool docs; load + query benchmarks (`scripts/loadtest`, `scripts/querybench`).
- **Distribution** (Phase 4): GoReleaser (binaries/deb/rpm), Helm chart, Heroku/Railway/DO/AWS-CFN manifests, deploy docs.
- **Scoped tokens**: `tr token create/ls/rm`; per-scope enforcement — `READ:<pipe>`, `APPEND:<ds>`, `ADMIN`, wildcards (ADR 0005). Frontends can hold a read-only pipe token.
- `TR_PIPE_RATE_LIMIT` (configurable per-token rate limit; 0 disables).

### Changed / Fixed
- **perf:** tuned ClickHouse HTTP transport (`MaxIdleConnsPerHost: 512`) — default (2) thrashed connections under load (p50 92ms + errors); pooled = ~19ms p50, ~0 errors (4–5×). Query latency now CH-bound: **single-flight p50 ~1.8ms** (CH ~0.35ms).

## [0.1.2] — 2026-06-29

### Added
- Marketing website deploy: static Next.js export served by nginx
  (`site/Dockerfile`), published as `ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/tiny-site` by the
  release workflow, and a `site` service in the prod compose (port 18080).
- API and website split across hosts: site at `tiny.ravencloak.org`, API at
  `tiny-api.ravencloak.org` (first-level subdomain — Cloudflare Universal SSL
  doesn't cover second-level like `api.tiny.*`).

## [0.1.1] — 2026-06-29

### Added
- One-click production stack `deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml` (ClickHouse 26.3 +
  Redis AOF + TinyRaven, health-gated, persistent volumes) for Dokploy Compose deploys.
- Docker image now bakes the `examples/quickstart` project at `/project` and
  defaults `TR_PROJECT_DIR=/project`, so a fresh deploy is queryable immediately.
- Marketing site under `site/` (Next.js + shadcn + Bklit charts): hero, features,
  one-env-var migration, illustrative price comparison, real benchmark
  (~177k events/s, p95 71ms — measured: 50 clients, batched, 1 node, 2.65M events
  persisted to ClickHouse).
- Deploy guide `docs/deploy/dokploy.md` + Cloudflare Tunnel config for `tiny.ravencloak.org`.

### Changed
- Release image is single-arch `linux/amd64` (x86) — dropped multi-arch/QEMU.

## [0.1.0] — 2026-06-29

First release: a working, self-hosted, Tinybird-API-compatible analytics backend
over OSS ClickHouse. Covers MILESTONE Phases 1 (MVP ingestion + query) and 2 (API
publishing + deployment). Single `tr` binary = server + CLI.

### Added — Phase 1 (MVP: core ingestion + query)
- HTTP server on `net/http` + `chi` router.
- `POST /v0/events` — JSON / NDJSON ingestion; per-row validate + quarantine
  (ADR 0018); `202 {successful_rows, quarantined_rows}` ack-on-buffer (ADR 0004).
- `GET /v0/pipes/{name}.json` — parameterized SQL pipes via `{{Type(name,default)}}`
  → ClickHouse `{name:Type}` parameters, injection-proof (ADR 0003).
- `GET /health` (liveness) + `GET /health/ready` (Redis + ClickHouse, cached) — ADR 0024.
- Gatherer: in-process batching, flush on `max(10,000 events, 5s)`, graceful drain.
- `.datasource` parser (SCHEMA/ENGINE/ENGINE_* + structural validation, ADR 0008/0027)
  → Redis schema registry (ADR 0001 — Redis-only metadata, no Postgres).
- `.pipe` parser + in-memory pipe registry with atomic hot-reload swap (ADR 0020).
- Bearer token auth middleware → Redis token store (ADR 0005).
- `tr local start` → Docker Compose (ClickHouse 26.3 LTS + Redis AOF + TinyRaven).
- Dev hot reload on `.datasource`/`.pipe` change (mtime poll).

### Added — Phase 2 (API publishing + deployment)
- `tr deploy` — validate all files, diff schema against live `system.columns`,
  create missing tables, apply additive `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN`; breaking changes
  detected and refused (shadow-table/`EXCHANGE` deferred to Phase 3, ADR 0007).
- Full pipe param types: `String`, `DateTime`, `Int64`, `Float64`, `UUID`,
  `Boolean` (+ `Int32`, `Date`, `DateTime64`) — validated + normalized, 400 on bad input.
- `GET /v0/sql` — read-only ClickHouse SQL proxy (`readonly=2` + caps, ADR 0011).
- `GET /v0/metrics` — Prometheus exposition via `prometheus/client_golang`.
- `GET /v0/openapi.json` — runtime OpenAPI 3.0 spec from the pipe registry (ADR 0017).
- Per-token rate limiting — in-memory `httprate` sliding window (ADR 0015).
- Query observability — `pipe_stats` table fed by a non-blocking background
  flusher (ADR 0014).
- Tinybird-compatible error envelope `{"error": "..."}` + status mapping +
  `X-DB-Exception-Code` passthrough (ADR 0012).

### Infrastructure
- CI (GitHub Actions): gofmt + `go vet`, race tests with coverage, integration
  tests against ClickHouse + Redis service containers, build; Codecov upload.
- CodeRabbit review config; Dockerfile (static `tr` binary).

### Known limitations (deliberate, see `// ponytail:` notes)
- Single-node `tr` (ADR 0031); ack-on-buffer is at-most-once on hard crash until WAL.
- `pipe_stats` lives in the workspace DB (not `tinybird.pipe_stats`); drops on overflow.
- Rate limit is a global per-token default; per-pipe `RATE_LIMIT` wiring pending.
- `/v0/sql` uses the `readonly=2` setting; a dedicated read-only CH user is the
  production upgrade.

content.text: Root cause: stock `nginx:alpine`, no `try_files` — `/use-cases` hits the RSC dir, 301s to `/use-cases/`, no index → 403. Fix = nginx conf with `try_files $uri $uri.html` (serves `use-cases.html`, kills the redirect+scheme-drop too). Adding conf, testing locally before re-tagging.

attachment.snippet:
1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to TinyRaven are documented here. Format follows
4	[Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/); this project adheres to
5	[Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
6	
7	## [0.3.4] — 2026-07-01
8	
9	### Fixed
10	- Marketing site nginx now serves Next static-export subroutes: `try_files $uri $uri.html` returns `/use-cases.html` for `/use-cases` instead of a dir 301 → 403 (the redirect also dropped https→http behind Cloudflare). Home page was unaffected.
11	
12	## [0.3.3] — 2026-07-01
13	
14	### Added
15	- Marketing site: `/use-cases` page mapping TinyRaven to Tinybird's eight documented use cases with honest coverage badges (Live / Via ClickHouse / Partial) and a demonstrated user-facing-dashboards spotlight using real demo output.
16	- `examples/dashboards-demo/`: reproducible drop-in demo (Tinybird dashboards use case) — `.datasource`/`.pipe` files, `run.sh` (Apple `container` local runtime), sample events, and verified receipts.
17	
18	### Fixed
19	- Nav section links now resolve from any route (`/#features` form) and expose the new Use cases page.
20	
21	### Known issues
22	- `tr deploy` cannot bootstrap a missing target database (native client pins the session to it → `UNKNOWN_DATABASE`); pre-create the DB. See #71. Prod compose is unaffected (ClickHouse image creates `tr_main`).
23	
24	## [0.3.2] — 2026-06-30
25	
26	### Changed
27	- Auto-deploy on release is now **version-tagged via the Dokploy API** (`compose.update` env → `compose.deploy`) instead of a webhook on `:latest`. A version tag is a new image reference, so Dokploy pulls the new digest every time; the `:latest` webhook recreated from cache and silently never updated.
28	
29	## [0.3.1] — 2026-06-30
30	
31	### Added
32	- API read endpoints toward full Tinybird `/v0` parity: `GET /v0/datasources`, `GET /v0/datasources/{name}`, `GET /v0/pipes`, `GET /v0/pipes/{name}` (all ADMIN), plus pipe output formats `GET /v0/pipes/{name}.{csv,ndjson}` (READ-scoped) alongside the existing `.json`.
33	- Distribution: signed APT + RPM repos published to GitHub Pages (`apt-get install tinyraven`); GoReleaser Scoop/AUR/Nix/winget publisher blocks (fail-safe — push only when their token exists); install matrix + `docs/install.md`.
34	- `docs/parity-gaps.md` ranked `/v0` parity audit.
35	
36	### Changed
37	- Prod deploy model: image tags pinned to `latest`; release builds `:version` + `:latest` then triggers the Dokploy deploy webhook (`pull_policy: always` recreates on the new digest). Dokploy panel env is the documented source of truth.
38	- APT/RPM gh-pages publish now does a scoped `rsync --delete` of `apt/`+`rpm/` (keep-2 pool prune actually removes old blobs); `helm-publish` + `apt-publish` share a concurrency group to avoid gh-pages races.
39	
40	### Tests
41	- `internal/apierr` and `internal/model` 0→100%; `internal/pipe` 86.7→96% (error-envelope mapping, `Token.HasScope` auth gate, registry hot-reload swap, control-flow branch + non-boolean-condition rejection).
42	
43	### Security
44	- Rotated the admin token (purged the old value from the Dokploy env and Redis `tr:token:`).
45	
46	## [0.3.0] — 2026-06-30
47	
48	### Added
49	- Control-flow pipe templating: `{% if/elif/else/end %}` + `defined()` via expr-lang (ADR 0003).
50	- Resource-token materialization: `TOKEN "x" READ/APPEND` in files → scoped tokens on `tr deploy` (ADR 0030).
51	- `tr deploy --check` (dry run), `tr login`, `tr status`.
52	- Per-pipe `RATE_LIMIT` enforcement (token+pipe; ADR 0015); `/tr/v1/docs` UI (off by default, `TR_DOCS_ENABLED`; ADR 0017); dedicated read-only ClickHouse user (ADR 0011).
53	- Release signing (GPG) + Helm chart published to GitHub Pages.
54	
55	### Changed
56	- ponytail cuts: strconv.Itoa over hand-rolled, merged CHPinger into Pinger, dropped dead Connector field.
57	
58	## [0.2.0] — 2026-06-29
59	
60	Phases 3–5 + scoped auth + a query-path perf fix.
61	
62	### Added
63	- **Branching** (Phase 3): `tr_{branch}` ClickHouse DB per git branch; `tr deploy --branch` / `tr local start --branch`.
64	- **Materialized views** + **breaking migrations** (shadow → backfill → `EXCHANGE TABLES`, `--allow-breaking`).
65	- **Config**: `~/.tinyraven/config.yml` (Tinybird-compatible) + `TINYBIRD_HOST/TOKEN/WORKSPACE` env.
66	- **Connectors** (Phase 5): Kafka/S3/PostgreSQL as ClickHouse-native engines in `.datasource` (ADR 0019) + templates; BI-tool docs; load + query benchmarks (`scripts/loadtest`, `scripts/querybench`).
67	- **Distribution** (Phase 4): GoReleaser (binaries/deb/rpm), Helm chart, Heroku/Railway/DO/AWS-CFN manifests, deploy docs.
68	- **Scoped tokens**: `tr token create/ls/rm`; per-scope enforcement — `READ:<pipe>`, `APPEND:<ds>`, `ADMIN`, wildcards (ADR 0005). Frontends can hold a read-only pipe token.
69	- `TR_PIPE_RATE_LIMIT` (configurable per-token rate limit; 0 disables).
70	
71	### Changed / Fixed
72	- **perf:** tuned ClickHouse HTTP transport (`MaxIdleConnsPerHost: 512`) — default (2) thrashed connections under load (p50 92ms + errors); pooled = ~19ms p50, ~0 errors (4–5×). Query latency now CH-bound: **single-flight p50 ~1.8ms** (CH ~0.35ms).
73	
74	## [0.1.2] — 2026-06-29
75	
76	### Added
77	- Marketing website deploy: static Next.js export served by nginx
78	  (`site/Dockerfile`), published as `ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/tiny-site` by the
79	  release workflow, and a `site` service in the prod compose (port 18080).
80	- API and website split across hosts: site at `tiny.ravencloak.org`, API at
81	  `tiny-api.ravencloak.org` (first-level subdomain — Cloudflare Universal SSL
82	  doesn't cover second-level like `api.tiny.*`).
83	
84	## [0.1.1] — 2026-06-29
85	
86	### Added
87	- One-click production stack `deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml` (ClickHouse 26.3 +
88	  Redis AOF + TinyRaven, health-gated, persistent volumes) for Dokploy Compose deploys.
89	- Docker image now bakes the `examples/quickstart` project at `/project` and
90	  defaults `TR_PROJECT_DIR=/project`, so a fresh deploy is queryable immediately.
91	- Marketing site under `site/` (Next.js + shadcn + Bklit charts): hero, features,
92	  one-env-var migration, illustrative price comparison, real benchmark
93	  (~177k events/s, p95 71ms — measured: 50 clients, batched, 1 node, 2.65M events
94	  persisted to ClickHouse).
95	- Deploy guide `docs/deploy/dokploy.md` + Cloudflare Tunnel config for `tiny.ravencloak.org`.
96	
97	### Changed
98	- Release image is single-arch `linux/amd64` (x86) — dropped multi-arch/QEMU.
99	
100	## [0.1.0] — 2026-06-29
101	
102	First release: a working, self-hosted, Tinybird-API-compatible analytics backend
103	over OSS ClickHouse. Covers MILESTONE Phases 1 (MVP ingestion + query) and 2 (API
104	publishing + deployment). Single `tr` binary = server + CLI.
105	
106	### Added — Phase 1 (MVP: core ingestion + query)
107	- HTTP server on `net/http` + `chi` router.
108	- `POST /v0/events` — JSON / NDJSON ingestion; per-row validate + quarantine
109	  (ADR 0018); `202 {successful_rows, quarantined_rows}` ack-on-buffer (ADR 0004).
110	- `GET /v0/pipes/{name}.json` — parameterized SQL pipes via `{{Type(name,default)}}`
111	  → ClickHouse `{name:Type}` parameters, injection-proof (ADR 0003).
112	- `GET /health` (liveness) + `GET /health/ready` (Redis + ClickHouse, cached) — ADR 0024.
113	- Gatherer: in-process batching, flush on `max(10,000 events, 5s)`, graceful drain.
114	- `.datasource` parser (SCHEMA/ENGINE/ENGINE_* + structural validation, ADR 0008/0027)
115	  → Redis schema registry (ADR 0001 — Redis-only metadata, no Postgres).
116	- `.pipe` parser + in-memory pipe registry with atomic hot-reload swap (ADR 0020).
117	- Bearer token auth middleware → Redis token store (ADR 0005).
118	- `tr local start` → Docker Compose (ClickHouse 26.3 LTS + Redis AOF + TinyRaven).
119	- Dev hot reload on `.datasource`/`.pipe` change (mtime poll).
120	
121	### Added — Phase 2 (API publishing + deployment)
122	- `tr deploy` — validate all files, diff schema against live `system.columns`,
123	  create missing tables, apply additive `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN`; breaking changes
124	  detected and refused (shadow-table/`EXCHANGE` deferred to Phase 3, ADR 0007).

... [28 lines truncated] ...

content.text:
Page is **live and fixed** — `https://tiny.ravencloak.org/use-cases` → 200 (watched it flip 301→502→200 as Dokploy swapped the image), content rendering, API healthy. v0.3.4 release green.

On the missing live-demo links — you're right, the page only links to the **GitHub** reproducible demo, not a clickable live endpoint. Reason: the demo I ran was **local + ephemeral** (my Mac, `localhost:8000`, torn down). Prod `tiny-api` currently serves the baked *quickstart* project, not the dashboards demo — and every pipe needs a token, so a bare public link 401s.

To get real clickable live links, two things needed:
1. Deploy the `dashboards-demo` project (web_events + top_pages + views_over_time) to prod + seed sample data.
2. A **public READ-scoped token** embedded in the link URLs (read-only, demo pipes only) — this is exactly how Tinybird's own docs demos work.

Step 2 is the one call worth your sign-off — it means a token visible in page source.

===docs: ADR 0030 token value===
# Resource tokens: declarative materialization on deploy, orphans warned not deleted
ADR 0005 says resource-scoped tokens are "declared in `.pipe`/`.datasource` and materialized on `tr deploy`." This pins down the flow.
- **Declaration (parity):** a `TOKEN "name" READ` line inside a `.pipe` (grants `PIPE:READ` on that pipe) or `.datasource` (`READ` / `APPEND`). The token **name** lives in git; the token **value** never does.
- **Materialize on deploy — idempotent upsert.** For each declared name, `tr deploy` looks it up in Redis (ADR 0001): absent → generate an opaque value (ADR 0005), store `value→scopes` + a `name→value` index, print the value **once** in deploy output. Present → keep the existing value. A redeploy **never rotates** a token's value — rotation would break every live client holding it.
- **Scopes are declarative — union across files.** A name declared in several files (`TOKEN "dashboard" READ` in pipe A and pipe B) is one token whose scope is the union of all its declarations, recomputed and re-applied on every deploy. Editing a file changes scope; the value is stable.
- **Value surface:** Redis holds the value; `tr token ls` and deploy output print it. Never written to git.
- **File-managed vs manual is a hard split.** Deploy reconciles **only** file-declared tokens. The bootstrap `ADMIN` token and anything from `tr token create` are manual — deploy never narrows, rotates, or prunes them.
- **Orphans are warned, not deleted.** A token whose name is declared in no file after a deploy is **orphaned**: deploy prints `token "X" no longer declared — run 'tr token rm X' to revoke` and **leaves it working**. Actual deletion is an explicit act: `tr token rm <name>` or `tr deploy --prune-tokens`.
- **Auto-revoke orphans (deploy deletes the Redis key when the declaration disappears)** — rejected as the default. It is "clean" and matches ADR 0005's instant-revocation, but it makes a typo or an accidental line deletion silently kill a live integration on the next deploy. Token deletion is destructive and should be a deliberate command, not a side effect of editing a file.
- **Rotate the value on every deploy** — rejected. Breaks all existing holders of the token; defeats the purpose of a stable resource token.

===docs: ADR 0030 token value===
# Resource tokens: declarative materialization on deploy, orphans warned not deleted
ADR 0005 says resource-scoped tokens are "declared in `.pipe`/`.datasource` and materialized on `tr deploy`." This pins down the flow.
- **Declaration (parity):** a `TOKEN "name" READ` line inside a `.pipe` (grants `PIPE:READ` on that pipe) or `.datasource` (`READ` / `APPEND`). The token **name** lives in git; the token **value** never does.
- **Materialize on deploy — idempotent upsert.** For each declared name, `tr deploy` looks it up in Redis (ADR 0001): absent → generate an opaque value (ADR 0005), store `value→scopes` + a `name→value` index, print the value **once** in deploy output. Present → keep the existing value. A redeploy **never rotates** a token's value — rotation would break every live client holding it.
- **Scopes are declarative — union across files.** A name declared in several files (`TOKEN "dashboard" READ` in pipe A and pipe B) is one token whose scope is the union of all its declarations, recomputed and re-applied on every deploy. Editing a file changes scope; the value is stable.
- **Value surface:** Redis holds the value; `tr token ls` and deploy output print it. Never written to git.
- **File-managed vs manual is a hard split.** Deploy reconciles **only** file-declared tokens. The bootstrap `ADMIN` token and anything from `tr token create` are manual — deploy never narrows, rotates, or prunes them.
- **Orphans are warned, not deleted.** A token whose name is declared in no file after a deploy is **orphaned**: deploy prints `token "X" no longer declared — run 'tr token rm X' to revoke` and **leaves it working**. Actual deletion is an explicit act: `tr token rm <name>` or `tr deploy --prune-tokens`.
- **Auto-revoke orphans (deploy deletes the Redis key when the declaration disappears)** — rejected as the default. It is "clean" and matches ADR 0005's instant-revocation, but it makes a typo or an accidental line deletion silently kill a live integration on the next deploy. Token deletion is destructive and should be a deliberate command, not a side effect of editing a file.
- **Rotate the value on every deploy** — rejected. Breaks all existing holders of the token; defeats the purpose of a stable resource token.

content.text:
Hit a real blocker worth surfacing before I build the wrong thing.

**Why it's not trivial:** a live prod demo link needs three things that don't exist on prod yet — the demo **tables** (prod `serve` only loads the registry, never creates tables; only `tr deploy` does), **seed data**, and a **read token**. And token values are random + never stored in git by design, so I can't pre-bake one into the static page. Plus I have no prod admin token or CH/Redis access from here (Dokploy-internal).

So it's one of two paths — your call:

questions.question: How should I get the dashboards demo live on prod (tables + data + token)?

options.label: Give me the prod admin token
options.description: You paste TR_ADMIN_TOKEN (or run 3 commands via `!`). I deploy the demo project to prod, seed data over the public ingest API, mint a READ-scoped token, capture its value, inject it into the page, rebuild+redeploy site. Fast — live in one pass. Manual, not reproducible, and shares a secret with me.
options.preview:
You: export TR_ADMIN_TOKEN=... (paste)
Me: tr deploy --project-dir demo (prod CH via tunnel)
    seed 300 events -> POST /v0/events
    tr token create --read top_pages ...
    -> capture value -> site lib/data -> retag
Live: tiny-api../v0/pipes/top_pages.json?token=...

options.label: Self-contained boot seed
options.description: Bake demo project into the image; add an entrypoint that runs `tr deploy` + seeds sample events on boot; add a `--value` flag to `tr token create` so the demo read-token has a fixed value committed in git (you OK'd token-in-page). Reproducible, survives redeploys, no secret sharing — but core code + compose change, bigger diff, one more release.
options.preview:
Dockerfile: COPY examples/dashboards-demo /project
entrypoint.sh: tr deploy --branch main
  && seed events && tr token create
     --read top_pages --value tr_demo_ro
  && exec tr serve
site: hardcode tr_demo_ro in links. Fully repeatable.

===API Dockerfile (final stage base)===
# Multi-stage build for the `tr` binary.
FROM golang:1.26-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /src
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download
COPY . .
ARG VERSION=dev
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -ldflags "-s -w -X main.version=${VERSION}" -o /out/tr ./cmd/tr

FROM alpine:3.20
RUN apk add --no-cache ca-certificates
COPY --from=build /out/tr /usr/local/bin/tr
# Ship a starter project so a fresh deploy has a working datasource + pipe.
# Replace /project (bind mount or your own COPY) with your real .datasource/.pipe.
COPY examples/quickstart /project
ENV TR_PROJECT_DIR=/project
EXPOSE 8000
ENTRYPOINT ["tr"]
CMD ["serve"]

content.text:
Seed live on `main` (300 rows). Here's the one block — run it on the **Dokploy host** (SSH in). It execs into the container, deploys the demo, seeds, and prints the read-only token:

```bash
CID=$(docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -i tinyraven | head -1)
echo "container: $CID"
docker exec -i "$CID" sh <<'SCRIPT'
set -e
apk add --no-cache curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
mkdir -p /tmp/demo
cat > /tmp/demo/web_events.datasource <<'EOF'
SCHEMA >
    timestamp DateTime,
    user_id String,
    path String,
    country String,
    device String

ENGINE "MergeTree"
ENGINE_SORTING_KEY "(path, timestamp)"
ENGINE_PARTITION_KEY "toYYYYMM(timestamp)"
EOF
cat > /tmp/demo/top_pages.pipe <<'EOF'
NODE endpoint
SQL >
    SELECT path, count() AS views, uniq(user_id) AS visitors
    FROM web_events
    GROUP BY path
    ORDER BY views DESC
    LIMIT {{Int32(limit, 10)}}
TYPE endpoint
EOF
cat > /tmp/demo/views_over_time.pipe <<'EOF'
NODE endpoint
SQL >
    SELECT toStartOfHour(timestamp) AS hour, count() AS views
    FROM web_events
    WHERE timestamp >= {{DateTime(start, '2020-01-01 00:00:00')}}
    GROUP BY hour
    ORDER BY hour
TYPE endpoint
EOF

tr deploy --project-dir /tmp/demo --branch main

curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ravencloak-org/tiny/main/examples/dashboards-demo/sample-events.ndjson -o /tmp/seed.ndjson
curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:8000/v0/events?name=web_events" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TR_ADMIN_TOKEN" --data-binary @/tmp/seed.ndjson; echo

echo "=== DEMO READ TOKEN (paste this back) ==="
tr token create --name demo_ro --read top_pages --read views_over_time | grep tr_
SCRIPT
```

Paste me the `tr_...` token it prints. Then I inject it into the page links, rebuild, retag → live clickable demo.

Two notes: assumes prod DB is `tr_main` (compose default — matches). If `docker ps` shows no `tinyraven` match, tell me the container name.

content.text:
`created` — good, but two things. Deploy made the **table + data**, but the running server serves **pipes from `/project` files (in-memory), not from Redis** — so `top_pages`/`views_over_time` aren't reachable yet (would 404). Confirmed at `internal/api/pipes.go:91` + `registry.go:11`.

One more command to make the pipes live (copies them into the watched `/project` dir → hot-reload picks them up):

```bash
CID=$(docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -i tinyraven | head -1)
docker exec "$CID" sh -c 'cp /tmp/demo/*.pipe /tmp/demo/*.datasource /project/ && ls /project'
```

Then give me **the `tr_...` token** the first block printed (the `demo_ro` value). With both, I'll verify the pipes answer on prod and wire the links.

(Note: `/project` is in-container → resets on next Dokploy redeploy. I'll bake the demo into the image after, so it survives. For now this gets it live.)

ssh -i "$HOME/.ssh/id_ed25519" -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=20 root@139.84.219.81 'bash -s' <<'OUTER'
CID=tiny-backend-btyefk-tinyraven-1
docker exec -i "$CID" sh <<'INNER'
set -e
apk add --no-cache curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
cat > /project/web_events.datasource <<'EOF'
SCHEMA >
    timestamp DateTime,
    user_id String,
    path String,
    country String,
    device String

ENGINE "MergeTree"
ENGINE_SORTING_KEY "(path, timestamp)"
ENGINE_PARTITION_KEY "toYYYYMM(timestamp)"
EOF
cat > /project/top_pages.pipe <<'EOF'
NODE endpoint
SQL >
    SELECT path, count() AS views, uniq(user_id) AS visitors
    FROM web_events
    GROUP BY path
    ORDER BY views DESC
    LIMIT {{Int32(limit, 10)}}
TYPE endpoint
EOF
cat > /project/views_over_time.pipe <<'EOF'
NODE endpoint
SQL >
    SELECT toStartOfHour(timestamp) AS hour, count() AS views
    FROM web_events
    WHERE timestamp >= {{DateTime(start, '2020-01-01 00:00:00')}}
    GROUP BY hour
    ORDER BY hour
TYPE endpoint
EOF
echo "--- files in /project ---"; ls /project
echo "--- deploy ---"
tr deploy --project-dir /project --branch main
echo "--- row count ---"
CNT=$(curl -s "http://localhost:8000/v0/sql?q=SELECT%20count()%20FROM%20web_events%20FORMAT%20TabSeparated" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TR_ADMIN_TOKEN")
echo "rows=$CNT"
if [ "$CNT" = "0" ] || [ -z "$CNT" ]; then
  echo "--- seeding ---"
  curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ravencloak-org/tiny/main/examples/dashboards-demo/sample-events.ndjson -o /tmp/seed.ndjson
  curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:8000/v0/events?name=web_events" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TR_ADMIN_TOKEN" --data-binary @/tmp/seed.ndjson; echo
fi
echo "--- (re)create demo_ro token ---"
tr token rm demo_ro >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
tr token create --name demo_ro --read top_pages --read views_over_time
INNER
OUTER

# Multi-stage build for the `tr` binary.
FROM golang:1.26-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /src
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download
COPY . .
ARG VERSION=dev
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -ldflags "-s -w -X main.version=${VERSION}" -o /out/tr ./cmd/tr

FROM alpine:3.20
RUN apk add --no-cache ca-certificates
COPY --from=build /out/tr /usr/local/bin/tr
# Ship a starter project so a fresh deploy has a working datasource + pipe.
# Replace /project (bind mount or your own COPY) with your real .datasource/.pipe.
COPY examples/quickstart /project
ENV TR_PROJECT_DIR=/project
EXPOSE 8000
ENTRYPOINT ["tr"]
CMD ["serve"]

content.text: Deploy running. Recording the prod-access facts + the pipe-registry gotcha to memory while it builds (non-obvious, will save time next prod task).

content.content:
1	---
2	name: tiny-prod-deployment
3	description: "TinyRaven production deployment topology + Cloudflare tunnel gotchas (Vultr VPS, Dokploy, cloudflared)"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 8800a864-e1cb-4d13-9744-9d16ef2a83c7
8	---
9	
10	TinyRaven runs in prod on a Vultr VPS (`root@139.84.219.81`, key `~/.ssh/id_ed25519`) via **Dokploy** (a Docker Compose app from `deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml`). Stack: `site` (nginx static, host port 18080) + `tinyraven` (API, host port 18000) + ClickHouse 26.3 + Redis (named volumes, persistent).
11	
12	**Public hosts (Cloudflare):**
13	- `https://tiny.ravencloak.org` → marketing **website** (→ localhost:18080)
14	- `https://tiny-api.ravencloak.org` → **API**, this is `TINYBIRD_HOST` (→ localhost:18000)
15	
16	**Tunnel:** exposed by the **caw** cloudflared tunnel (`c3308fe1-…`), managed by **systemd** `cloudflared-caw.service`, config `/root/.cloudflared/config.yml`. That one tunnel also serves caw.ravencloak.org + the viewrr.stream hosts — **be careful editing its config**. A second tunnel `dokploy` (`cloudflared-dokploy.service`, `dokploy.yml`) serves dokploy.jobin.wtf.
17	
18	**Hard-won gotchas:**
19	- **cloudflared here EXITS on SIGHUP (does not hot-reload).** Plus `Restart=on-failure` ignores a clean exit. So `kill -HUP` took the tunnel DOWN. To apply config changes: edit config.yml then **`systemctl restart cloudflared-caw`** — never SIGHUP.
20	- **Cloudflare Universal SSL covers first-level subdomains only** (`*.ravencloak.org`). Second-level like `api.tiny.ravencloak.org` → TLS handshake failure (needs paid ACM). That's why the API is `tiny-api.` not `api.tiny.`.
21	- `pgrep -f "run caw"` matches your own ssh shell (false positive) — use `pgrep -x cloudflared` / `ps -C cloudflared`.
22	- The API has **no `/` route** (API-first) → `/` returns 404 by design; health is `/health` (NOT `/ready`).
23	- **Cloudflare bot-fight returns `403 error code: 1010` to non-browser User-Agents** (python urllib, curl libs). Even unauthed `/health` gets 1010. Fix when scripting against prod: send a real browser `User-Agent` header. Real Tinybird SDK clients may trip this → may need a CF UA/WAF allowlist rule.
24	
25	**Dokploy env is the source of truth — it CLOBBERS repo `deploy/.env`:** Dokploy stores the compose env in its postgres (`docker exec dokploy-postgres.* psql -U dokploy -d dokploy`, table `compose`, row `name='backend'`, column `env`). At **every** deploy it rewrites `/etc/dokploy/compose/tiny-backend-btyefk/code/deploy/.env` from that DB value, overwriting whatever git committed. So the repo `deploy/.env` version-pin is **cosmetic** — prod silently reverted to an old `TINYRAVEN_TAG` once because the DB env still pinned it. **Version bumps + secrets must be changed in the Dokploy DB env (or UI), not repo `.env`.** Edit DB durably via `UPDATE compose SET env=convert_from(decode('<base64>','base64'),'UTF8') WHERE name='backend'`, then recreate: `cd code/deploy && DOCKER_CONFIG=/root/.docker docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml -p tiny-backend-btyefk up -d --no-deps --pull always tinyraven site`. **Always pass `-f docker-compose.prod.yml`** — bare `docker compose up` walks to the root dev compose ("no such service: site").
26	
27	**Token rotation needs TWO places:** the admin token persists in **both** Dokploy DB env (`TR_ADMIN_TOKEN`) **and** Redis under `tr:token:<value>`. Restarting with a new env token only ADDS the new key; the old one keeps validating until you `redis-cli DEL tr:token:<oldvalue>` (redis container `tiny-backend-btyefk-redis-1`, no auth). Datasource registry keys are `tr:ds:<name>`. Current admin token saved root-only at `/root/.tinyraven_admin_token` (prod) + `~/.config/tinyraven/prod-admin.env` (local).
28	
29	**Images:** `ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/{tiny,tiny-site}:vX.Y.Z` + `:latest`, **public**, **linux/amd64 only** (user wants x86; local M-series builds arm64 itself).
30	
31	**Auto-deploy on release (WORKING, version-tagged Dokploy API):** push tag `vX.Y.Z` → `.github/workflows/release.yml` builds both images, creates GH release, then the `deploy-dokploy` job POSTs the Dokploy API: `compose.update` (sets env `TINYRAVEN_TAG`/`SITE_TAG`=tag, rebuilt from secrets) then `compose.deploy`. A **version** tag is a new image ref → Dokploy always pulls the new digest. **The webhook+`:latest` path FAILED** — Dokploy's webhook redeploy recreates from the *cached* `:latest` and never pulls (even with `pull_policy: always`), so prod silently stayed on the old version. Version-tag is the fix.
32	- Dokploy panel/API: **`https://dokploy.jobin.wtf`** (public; auth header `x-api-key: <key>`). composeId **`HHL9RZlnr5lErt91OJjwQ`** (name `backend`). Routes: `compose.update` (REPLACES the whole `env` blob — must resend `TR_ADMIN_TOKEN`+tags), `compose.deploy`, `compose.one`, `compose.all`.
33	- GH secrets: `DOKPLOY_TOKEN` (Dokploy api key — better-auth stores it HASHED in DB table `apikey.key`, NOT recoverable), `TR_ADMIN_TOKEN` (so CI can rebuild the env blob). `DOKPLOY_DEPLOY_WEBHOOK` now unused.
34	- Manual deploy still works: set DB env tag (see above) + `docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml -p tiny-backend-btyefk up -d --no-deps --pull always tinyraven site`.
35	
36	See [[parallel-agent-orchestration]].
37

---
name: tiny-prod-deployment
description: "TinyRaven production deployment topology + Cloudflare tunnel gotchas (Vultr VPS, Dokploy, cloudflared)"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 8800a864-e1cb-4d13-9744-9d16ef2a83c7
---

TinyRaven runs in prod on a Vultr VPS (`root@139.84.219.81`, key `~/.ssh/id_ed25519`) via **Dokploy** (a Docker Compose app from `deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml`). Stack: `site` (nginx static, host port 18080) + `tinyraven` (API, host port 18000) + ClickHouse 26.3 + Redis (named volumes, persistent).

**Public hosts (Cloudflare):**
- `https://tiny.ravencloak.org` → marketing **website** (→ localhost:18080)
- `https://tiny-api.ravencloak.org` → **API**, this is `TINYBIRD_HOST` (→ localhost:18000)

**Tunnel:** exposed by the **caw** cloudflared tunnel (`c3308fe1-…`), managed by **systemd** `cloudflared-caw.service`, config `/root/.cloudflared/config.yml`. That one tunnel also serves caw.ravencloak.org + the viewrr.stream hosts — **be careful editing its config**. A second tunnel `dokploy` (`cloudflared-dokploy.service`, `dokploy.yml`) serves dokploy.jobin.wtf.

**Hard-won gotchas:**
- **cloudflared here EXITS on SIGHUP (does not hot-reload).** Plus `Restart=on-failure` ignores a clean exit. So `kill -HUP` took the tunnel DOWN. To apply config changes: edit config.yml then **`systemctl restart cloudflared-caw`** — never SIGHUP.
- **Cloudflare Universal SSL covers first-level subdomains only** (`*.ravencloak.org`). Second-level like `api.tiny.ravencloak.org` → TLS handshake failure (needs paid ACM). That's why the API is `tiny-api.` not `api.tiny.`.
- `pgrep -f "run caw"` matches your own ssh shell (false positive) — use `pgrep -x cloudflared` / `ps -C cloudflared`.
- The API has **no `/` route** (API-first) → `/` returns 404 by design; health is `/health` (NOT `/ready`).
- **Cloudflare bot-fight returns `403 error code: 1010` to non-browser User-Agents** (python urllib, curl libs). Even unauthed `/health` gets 1010. Fix when scripting against prod: send a real browser `User-Agent` header. Real Tinybird SDK clients may trip this → may need a CF UA/WAF allowlist rule.

**Dokploy env is the source of truth — it CLOBBERS repo `deploy/.env`:** Dokploy stores the compose env in its postgres (`docker exec dokploy-postgres.* psql -U dokploy -d dokploy`, table `compose`, row `name='backend'`, column `env`). At **every** deploy it rewrites `/etc/dokploy/compose/tiny-backend-btyefk/code/deploy/.env` from that DB value, overwriting whatever git committed. So the repo `deploy/.env` version-pin is **cosmetic** — prod silently reverted to an old `TINYRAVEN_TAG` once because the DB env still pinned it. **Version bumps + secrets must be changed in the Dokploy DB env (or UI), not repo `.env`.** Edit DB durably via `UPDATE compose SET env=convert_from(decode('<base64>','base64'),'UTF8') WHERE name='backend'`, then recreate: `cd code/deploy && DOCKER_CONFIG=/root/.docker docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml -p tiny-backend-btyefk up -d --no-deps --pull always tinyraven site`. **Always pass `-f docker-compose.prod.yml`** — bare `docker compose up` walks to the root dev compose ("no such service: site").

**Token rotation needs TWO places:** the admin token persists in **both** Dokploy DB env (`TR_ADMIN_TOKEN`) **and** Redis under `tr:token:<value>`. Restarting with a new env token only ADDS the new key; the old one keeps validating until you `redis-cli DEL tr:token:<oldvalue>` (redis container `tiny-backend-btyefk-redis-1`, no auth). Datasource registry keys are `tr:ds:<name>`. Current admin token saved root-only at `/root/.tinyraven_admin_token` (prod) + `~/.config/tinyraven/prod-admin.env` (local).

**Images:** `ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/{tiny,tiny-site}:vX.Y.Z` + `:latest`, **public**, **linux/amd64 only** (user wants x86; local M-series builds arm64 itself).

**Auto-deploy on release (WORKING, version-tagged Dokploy API):** push tag `vX.Y.Z` → `.github/workflows/release.yml` builds both images, creates GH release, then the `deploy-dokploy` job POSTs the Dokploy API: `compose.update` (sets env `TINYRAVEN_TAG`/`SITE_TAG`=tag, rebuilt from secrets) then `compose.deploy`. A **version** tag is a new image ref → Dokploy always pulls the new digest. **The webhook+`:latest` path FAILED** — Dokploy's webhook redeploy recreates from the *cached* `:latest` and never pulls (even with `pull_policy: always`), so prod silently stayed on the old version. Version-tag is the fix.
- Dokploy panel/API: **`https://dokploy.jobin.wtf`** (public; auth header `x-api-key: <key>`). composeId **`HHL9RZlnr5lErt91OJjwQ`** (name `backend`). Routes: `compose.update` (REPLACES the whole `env` blob — must resend `TR_ADMIN_TOKEN`+tags), `compose.deploy`, `compose.one`, `compose.all`.
- GH secrets: `DOKPLOY_TOKEN` (Dokploy api key — better-auth stores it HASHED in DB table `apikey.key`, NOT recoverable), `TR_ADMIN_TOKEN` (so CI can rebuild the env blob). `DOKPLOY_DEPLOY_WEBHOOK` now unused.
- Manual deploy still works: set DB env tag (see above) + `docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml -p tiny-backend-btyefk up -d --no-deps --pull always tinyraven site`.

See [[parallel-agent-orchestration]].

input.new_string:
**Pipe registry is in-memory from `TR_PROJECT_DIR` files, NOT Redis.** `tr deploy` creates the CH table + writes the Redis registry, but the running `serve` only serves pipes it loaded from `/project` (hot-reload = mtime poll on that dir; `internal/api/pipes.go` → `PipeReg.Get`, `internal/pipe/registry.go` in-memory). So to expose a NEW pipe on prod you must land the `.pipe` file in `/project` (write it there or bake into the image) — `tr deploy` alone leaves it 404 on the HTTP API. Container name pattern: `tiny-backend-btyefk-tinyraven-1`; `$TR_ADMIN_TOKEN` is already env inside it (no need to fetch). Note: default-UA `curl` to `tiny-api` `/v0/pipes/*` worked fine (200) — the CF 1010 bot-fight did not trip on those paths this session.

**Live dashboards demo on prod (from 2026-07-01):** `/use-cases` page has clickable "Try it live" links → `tiny-api.ravencloak.org/v0/pipes/{top_pages,views_over_time}.json?token=…`. Backed by a read-only token `demo_ro` (scope `READ:top_pages,READ:views_over_time`; 403 on `/v0/sql`) over a `web_events` datasource seeded with 300 rows. The 3 demo files (`web_events.datasource`, `top_pages.pipe`, `views_over_time.pipe`) are baked into the API image `/project` (Dockerfile) so pipes survive redeploys; table+data persist in CH volume, token in Redis AOF. `?token=` query-param auth works (needed for clickable links). Deploy can't bootstrap its own DB (issue #71) but prod is unaffected (CH image creates `tr_main`).

See [[parallel-agent-orchestration]].

---
name: tiny-prod-deployment
description: "TinyRaven production deployment topology + Cloudflare tunnel gotchas (Vultr VPS, Dokploy, cloudflared)"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 8800a864-e1cb-4d13-9744-9d16ef2a83c7
---

TinyRaven runs in prod on a Vultr VPS (`root@139.84.219.81`, key `~/.ssh/id_ed25519`) via **Dokploy** (a Docker Compose app from `deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml`). Stack: `site` (nginx static, host port 18080) + `tinyraven` (API, host port 18000) + ClickHouse 26.3 + Redis (named volumes, persistent).

**Public hosts (Cloudflare):**
- `https://tiny.ravencloak.org` → marketing **website** (→ localhost:18080)
- `https://tiny-api.ravencloak.org` → **API**, this is `TINYBIRD_HOST` (→ localhost:18000)

**Tunnel:** exposed by the **caw** cloudflared tunnel (`c3308fe1-…`), managed by **systemd** `cloudflared-caw.service`, config `/root/.cloudflared/config.yml`. That one tunnel also serves caw.ravencloak.org + the viewrr.stream hosts — **be careful editing its config**. A second tunnel `dokploy` (`cloudflared-dokploy.service`, `dokploy.yml`) serves dokploy.jobin.wtf.

**Hard-won gotchas:**
- **cloudflared here EXITS on SIGHUP (does not hot-reload).** Plus `Restart=on-failure` ignores a clean exit. So `kill -HUP` took the tunnel DOWN. To apply config changes: edit config.yml then **`systemctl restart cloudflared-caw`** — never SIGHUP.
- **Cloudflare Universal SSL covers first-level subdomains only** (`*.ravencloak.org`). Second-level like `api.tiny.ravencloak.org` → TLS handshake failure (needs paid ACM). That's why the API is `tiny-api.` not `api.tiny.`.
- `pgrep -f "run caw"` matches your own ssh shell (false positive) — use `pgrep -x cloudflared` / `ps -C cloudflared`.
- The API has **no `/` route** (API-first) → `/` returns 404 by design; health is `/health` (NOT `/ready`).
- **Cloudflare bot-fight returns `403 error code: 1010` to non-browser User-Agents** (python urllib, curl libs). Even unauthed `/health` gets 1010. Fix when scripting against prod: send a real browser `User-Agent` header. Real Tinybird SDK clients may trip this → may need a CF UA/WAF allowlist rule.

**Token rotation needs TWO places:** the admin token persists in **both** Dokploy DB env (`TR_ADMIN_TOKEN`) **and** Redis under `tr:token:<value>`. Restarting with a new env token only ADDS the new key; the old one keeps validating until you `redis-cli DEL tr:token:<oldvalue>` (redis container `tiny-backend-btyefk-redis-1`, no auth). Datasource registry keys are `tr:ds:<name>`. Current admin token saved root-only at `/root/.tinyraven_admin_token` (prod) + `~/.config/tinyraven/prod-admin.env` (local).

**Images:** `ghcr.io/ravencloak-org/{tiny,tiny-site}:vX.Y.Z` + `:latest`, **public**, **linux/amd64 only** (user wants x86; local M-series builds arm64 itself).

content.text:
Short answer: Timescale would make it **slower**, not faster — and the caching you're describing already exists two ways. Premise is backwards.

**ClickHouse already IS the fast append-only time-series store.** It's columnar OLAP built for exactly this: append-heavy, immutable, `GROUP BY toStartOfHour(...)` scans. TimescaleDB is Postgres-with-hypertables — row-oriented at heart, compression bolted on. On the dashboard query shape (large-scan aggregations), CH beats Timescale in basically every public benchmark. Putting Timescale "in front" of CH to speed up reads caches a fast thing behind a slower thing.

**The "cache append-only rollups" job is already done by materialized views.** That's the native mechanism. MV pre-aggregates on ingest (incremental) → the dashboard hits a tiny pre-rolled table, not the raw 300M rows. Append-only data that "keeps appending like WAL" → MV rolls each new part forward automatically. `.pipe` files support `MATERIALIZATION` / `TARGET_TABLE` for exactly this. No second DB.

**Repeated identical queries → Redis result cache.** Already the design: Redis holds token validation, rate-limit counters, **and query results** (pipes carry `CACHE_TTL`). Hot dashboard query served from Redis, skips CH entirely.

**Why not add Timescale anyway:**
- Constraint: **no PostgreSQL** (ADR 0001, Redis-only metadata). Timescale = dragging a whole Postgres in.
- Doubles the data + a sync pipeline (CH↔Timescale), two sources of truth, replication lag → dashboards show stale/inconsistent numbers.
- Tinybird itself is ClickHouse-only. Adding Timescale breaks the parity thesis (ScyllaDB→Cassandra, same engine).
- Ops: a second stateful system to back up, tune, fail over. For zero read-speed win.

So the caching ladder is already: **MV (pre-aggregate) → Redis (result TTL) → CH (raw)**. Timescale slots nowhere better than what's there. If a specific query is slow, the fix is an MV or a Redis TTL, not a new database.

Want me to add a short "why not a second DB / caching model" note to the ADRs or the use-cases page? And — the v0.3.5 site deploy is still finishing; I'll confirm the live links when it lands.

content.text: Deploy green. Final verification — page live links + click-through + confirming the redeploy didn't drop the pipes:

input.command:
REDACTED
echo "=== site page shows live links? (wait for dokploy swap) ==="
for i in $(seq 1 10); do
  hit=$(curl -sL -m8 https://tiny.ravencloak.org/use-cases | grep -c "top_pages.json?limit=5&token=$TOK")
  echo "attempt $i: link-present=$hit"; [ "$hit" != "0" ] && break; sleep 12
done
echo "=== click-through the exact link on the page → real JSON? ==="

**Live and verified end-to-end.** 🐦

`https://tiny.ravencloak.org/use-cases` — "Try it live" links render and click through to real prod JSON:
- `top_pages.json` → `/blog/launch:88, /:74, /docs:54, /pricing:42, /features:25`
- `views_over_time.json` → 10 hourly buckets
- Survived the redeploy (baked image, 200) — durable.

**Full chain shipped (v0.3.3→v0.3.5):**
- Marketing `/use-cases` page — 8 use cases, honest badges (5 live / 3 caveated), dashboards spotlight with real receipt.
- Nginx `try_files` fix (subroute 403 → 200).
- Prod dashboards demo: `web_events` + 300 rows + `top_pages`/`views_over_time` pipes on `tiny-api`, read-only `demo_ro` token (`?token=` works, `/v0/sql`→403), baked into the image for durability.
- Bug #71 filed (deploy DB bootstrap).

**Timescale:** covered last turn — no, it'd cache a fast store behind a slow one; MV + Redis TTL already do that job; violates no-Postgres + parity.

Loose ends:
- Local demo containers still on your Mac (`tr-ch`/`tr-redis` + `tr serve` pid 78523) — `container rm -f tr-ch tr-redis` to clear.
- Token `tr_rulT...` is public in page source by design (read-only). Rotate anytime: `docker exec tiny-backend-btyefk-tinyraven-1 tr token rm demo_ro` then recreate + bump the site.

Want the "why not a second DB" caching note added to the ADRs/page, or call it done?

===ADRs (highest number)===
0028-cicd-template-pr-ephemeral-branch.md
0029-api-versioning-v0-frozen-tr-namespace-native.md
0030-resource-token-materialization-on-deploy.md
0031-single-node-scope-no-tr-horizontal-ha.md
0032-drop-kin-openapi-and-viper.md
0033-single-node-and-replication-roadmap.md
===format of a recent ADR===
# Redis is the only metadata store; no PostgreSQL

TinyRaven stores all metadata — workspace state, pipe/datasource definitions, tokens, and deploy state — in **Redis** (AOF-persisted, mounted volume). ClickHouse holds the actual event data. There is no PostgreSQL, SQLite, or pluggable-backend matrix.

## Why

- **Tinybird does exactly this.** Tinybird uses Redis for workspace/pipe/token metadata and ClickHouse for data — no relational metadata DB. Matching it serves the parity goal and is a proven design.
- **Zero new dependency.** Redis is already in the stack (token cache, rate limiting). Postgres would add a second stateful service to every deploy path (Heroku add-on, AWS RDS, Compose/Helm container) — directly undercutting the "minimal infra / pay only infrastructure" positioning.
- **Git is the source of truth for definitions.** `.datasource` and `.pipe` files live in the user's repo. The metadata store is a *registry of what has been deployed*, rebuildable by re-running `tr deploy`. So definitions don't need transactional durability — only tokens and deploy state do, which Redis persistence covers.

## Consequences

- Redis runs as a **system of record**, not a throwaway cache: AOF persistence + a mounted volume are mandatory in every deploy template.
- No cross-store SQL transaction during `tr deploy`. Partial-deploy failures are handled by idempotent re-apply (re-run `tr deploy` from git), not rollback. ClickHouse DDL isn't transactional anyway, so this changes nothing in practice.

## Considered and rejected

- **PostgreSQL (prod) + SQLite (local) split** — two backends to build/test/document for an MVP; and the "local needs no server" premise fails because `tr local start` already runs Redis + ClickHouse in Compose.
- **ParadeDB** — a Postgres BM25/search extension; no metadata workload here needs full-text search.

input.content:
# No second analytical/time-series store; read acceleration is materialized views + Redis

TinyRaven does not add TimescaleDB, DuckDB, a search engine, or any second store to "speed up" or "cache" reads in front of ClickHouse. Read acceleration has exactly two layers, both already in the stack:

1. **Materialized views** — pre-aggregate on ingest. Append-only event data rolls forward incrementally into small target tables; dashboard queries hit the rollup, not the raw rows. Declared in `.pipe` files (`MATERIALIZATION` / `TARGET_TABLE`).
2. **Redis result cache** — repeated identical pipe queries served from Redis with a per-pipe `CACHE_TTL`, skipping ClickHouse entirely.

Ladder: **MV (pre-aggregate) → Redis (result TTL) → ClickHouse (raw)**.

## Why

- **ClickHouse is already the fast append-only time-series store.** Columnar OLAP built for `GROUP BY toStartOfHour(...)` over large immutable scans. TimescaleDB is Postgres-with-hypertables — row-oriented at heart; on the dashboard aggregation shape it loses to ClickHouse in public benchmarks. Putting Timescale "in front" of CH caches a fast store behind a slower one.
- **The append-only-rollup job is what materialized views are for.** Data that "keeps appending like a WAL and never changes" is the ideal MV input — each new part rolls forward automatically. That is the cache; it needs no second database.
- **A second store doubles the data and adds a sync pipeline.** CH↔Timescale replication means two sources of truth and lag — dashboards would show stale/inconsistent numbers. A result cache (Redis, TTL-bounded) has bounded, understood staleness; a mirrored DB does not.
- **No PostgreSQL (ADR 0001).** Timescale is a Postgres extension — it drags a whole Postgres into every deploy path, undercutting the minimal-infra positioning.
- **Parity.** Tinybird is ClickHouse-only (its speed-up primitive is materialized pipes, not a second engine). Matching that keeps the drop-in thesis intact.

## Consequences

- A slow pipe is fixed by adding a materialized view or a `CACHE_TTL`, never by introducing a new backend.
- The only stateful services remain ClickHouse (data) and Redis (metadata + hot cache) — see ADR 0001.

## Considered and rejected

- **TimescaleDB as a read cache / mirror** — slower than CH for these queries, adds Postgres (violates ADR 0001), introduces a sync pipeline and a second source of truth for zero read-speed gain.
- **DuckDB / embedded OLAP as a query-side accelerator** — no workload here that CH + MV + Redis doesn't already cover; adds a second query engine to reason about.
